sentences.text: This chapter describes the RV32E and RV64E base integer instruction sets, designed for microcontrollers in embedded systems. RV32E and RV64E are reduced versions of RV32I and RV64I, respectively: the only change is to reduce the number of integer registers to 16.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV32E and RV64E Base Integer Instruction Sets, Version 2.0

sentences.text: This chapter only outlines the differences between RV32E/RV64E and RV32I/RV64I, and so should be read after and .
sentences.section: Preamble > RV32E and RV64E Base Integer Instruction Sets, Version 2.0

sentences.text: RV32E and RV64E reduce the integer register count to 16 general-purpose registers, (`x0-x15`), where `x0` is a dedicated zero register.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV32E and RV64E Base Integer Instruction Sets, Version 2.0 > RV32E and RV64E Programmers’ Model

sentences.text: RV32E and RV64E use the same instruction-set encoding as RV32I and RV64I respectively, except that only registers `x0-x15` are provided.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV32E and RV64E Base Integer Instruction Sets, Version 2.0 > RV32E and RV64E Instruction Set Encoding

sentences.text: All encodings specifying the other registers `x16-x31` are reserved.
sentences.section: Preamble > RV32E and RV64E Base Integer Instruction Sets, Version 2.0 > RV32E and RV64E Instruction Set Encoding
